fullArticle:

The Honolulu Museum of Art (HoMA) has announced David Odo as its new director. Odo, who specializes in nineteenth-century photography of Japan joins the institution from the Georgia Museum of Art at the University of Georgia in Athens, where he is director and chief curator. He succeeds Halona Norton-Westbrook, who departed last summer to helm the Modern Art Museum of Fort Worth. He will step into his new role on September 1.

Under Odo’s leadership, the Georgia Museum of Art saw annual attendance rise to 61,000, a record for the institution, with overall revenue increasing by 141 percent. Prior roles include a top pos...
